content_hints.description: The gate does not open so much as permit. One moment you are on Level 22, and the next you are somewhere else entirely -- standing on a flagstone floor that stretches in every direction beyond the reach of torchlight. The ceiling is not stone. It is sky -- a night sky filled with stars in constellations you do not recognize, turning slowly above you like a planetarium built by a god with a very specific aesthetic.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You step onto a flagstone floor stretching in every direction beyond the reach of your light. There is no ceiling, only a vast, cold night sky filled with stars in constellations you have never seen. The air smells of ozone and old paper, crackling faintly against your skin.

hazards.name: Reality instability (wild magic zone, roll on surge table)

event_triggers: On entry: The sign rotates to show its reverse side: 'JUST KIDDING. THERE IS NO END. ONLY DEEPER.'

content_hints.description: The corridor is a 20-foot-high, 20-foot-wide stone corridor ending before an adamantine double door bearing Dwarvish runes that reads, “THETHREE KEYS. BRING THEM FORTH.”
content_hints.read_aloud: You step into a corridor that bends in ways that corridors should not. Doors line both sides—red, blue, green, black, gold—each one slightly ajar. Through the gaps, you catch impossible glimpses: a sunlit beach, a city wreathed in fire, a corridor identical to this one where figures that look disturbingly like you peer back through the crack.
event_triggers: On wrong door: You step through and emerge from a different door in the same corridor. Halaster's laughter echoes from everywhere and nowhere.
On correct door (DC 17 Arcana to identify): The geometry resolves. The corridor straightens. For one merciful moment, physics works as intended.

content_hints.description: The Halaster Lair is situated in a small cave with a 10-foot-high ceiling and a 20-foot-deep pool containing a crevasse at the bottom, from which cold water bubbles up from a natural spring. The thralls of the Ssethian Scourge use the sightless cave fish that swim here as a source of food.
content_hints.read_aloud: > Mirrors line the circular gallery wall, each one taller than a man, each framed in tarnished silver etched with spiraling sigils. Your reflection stares back from the nearest—but it is wrong. Younger, or older, or wearing an expression you are not making. The next mirror shows only the empty room behind you, as if you are not standing here at all.

loot.name: Mirror Shard (can cast Scrying 1/week when reassembled)

hazards.name: Mirror of Opposition (creates hostile duplicate on eye contact)

event_triggers: On empty mirror examined: Your reflection appears after a delay, mouths the word 'RUN,' then vanishes.
On marble taken: Every mirror in the gallery cracks simultaneously. The fragments do not fall -- they hover, showing a thousand tiny reflections of your face, each one wearing a different expression.

content_hints.description: The iron chest behind the throne has an arcane lock spell cast on it that Halaster alone can ignore.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see leather-bound spellbooks bearing Halaster's personal rune, collectively containing every spell on the wizard's spell list in the Player's Handbook. The scent of old parchment hangs thick in the air, layered over something sharper—ink that is still wet, pages that are still being written.

loot.name: Tome of Clear Thought (guarded by the Arcanaloth)
Halaster's Diary (latest volume, 3,000 years of entries)

hazards.name: Trapped books (various spells trigger on opening)
Caged books (release causes psychic damage in 30-ft radius)

event_triggers: On Arcanaloth encountered: It is reading. It looks up with an expression of profound annoyance. 'I am on page 14,672. I have been reading for nine years. This had better be important.'

On diary read: The final entry reads: 'They are coming. I know because I invited them. I know because I am them. I know because I already wrote this. Madness is not the absence of reason -- it is reason taken to its conclusion. See you at the Throne.'

content_hints.description: A chamber dominated by a crystalline engine the size of a house, its facets humming with contained power. Arcane energy flows through diamond conduits in visible rivers of blue-white light, and the air crackles with so much raw magic that your hair stands on end and metal objects vibrate. This is the Spell Engine -- Halaster's masterwork, the device that maintains every ward, every trap, every teleportation circle, every twisted geometry in all twenty-three levels of Undermountain. It has been running for a thousand years.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a crystalline structure the size of a house dominating the chamber, its facets humming with a sound that bypasses your ears and resonates directly in your chest. Rivers of blue-white light flow through diamond conduits, branching and merging in patterns too complex to follow. The air is alive with raw magic.

loot.name: Spell Engine Component (priceless, dangerous to remove)

hazards.name: Arcane discharge (3d10 force damage per round within 10 ft)

event_triggers: On engine tampered: Halaster's voice booms through the level: 'DO NOT TOUCH THAT. I spent four hundred years calibrating the phase alignment. If you break it, REALITY breaks. And then I have to FIX reality, and that puts me in a VERY bad mood.'
On engine studied (DC 20 Arcana): Understanding the engine grants insight into Halaster's defenses. All DCs in the final chamber are reduced by 2.

content_hints.description: The defense of the island is a minefield of skeletons that rush up from the sand, detectable by noise, vibrations caused by movement, and pressure on the sand. When pirates landed here, the skeletons made them pay dearly, but the pirates eventually cleared the area.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the exit, thirty feet ahead, plainly visible. Something invisible stops you mid-stride—a wall of nothing, solid as stone, that redirects you sideways into a corridor you did not choose. The floor ahead looks solid, but your thrown pebble falls through it with a distant clatter.

hazards.name: Invisible walls (Intelligence or Investigation to navigate)
Illusory floor (3d6 falling into spiked pit)

event_triggers: On first failure: 'Oh dear. That looked painful. Would you like a hint? Too bad. I do not give hints. Well, maybe one: the floor is lying to you.'
On success: 'Impressive. Most die on the third reconfiguration. You made it through in -- let me check -- seven minutes. New record. I am almost disappointed.'

content_hints.description: A dais of black marble floats unsupported in a void of stars, with galaxies wheeling in the distance. The throne is carved from a single meteorite, its surface still bearing the heat scars of atmospheric entry.
content_hints.read_aloud: > You see a stone pillar carved with a small symbol: a perfect circle with ten equidistant spokes radiating outward from its circumference. In the middle of this circle is a smaller circular indentation that bears a passing resemblance to a lidless eye.

hazards.name: Void edge (Dexterity save or fall into infinite space)
Cosmic radiation (1d6 radiant per round without cover)

event_triggers: On throne sat upon: For one instant, you see all twenty-three levels of Undermountain simultaneously -- every room, every creature, every trap. The vision lasts a heartbeat and leaves you with a nosebleed and a headache that will not fade for days.
On void examined: Something moves in the stars. Something impossibly large and impossibly far away. It is coming closer. It has been coming closer for a very long time.

content_hints.description: The sanctum of Halaster Blackcloak is not a room. It is a statement. The walls are living spellwork -- formulae that rewrite themselves in real time, adapting to threats, learning from attacks, evolving.
content_hints.read_aloud: You see the walls alive, arcane formulae crawling across every surface in luminous script, rewriting themselves in real time, adapting and evolving to your presence. The floor beneath your feet is a mosaic of staggering detail, depicting a vast dungeon in miniature, and as you watch, tiny figures that look like you move through its corridors. At the chamber's center, within a circle of runes that burn white-hot, a gaunt figure stands in star-scarred robes, his long beard braided with what appears to be spell components. His eyes, one brown, one silver, find yours with a lucidity that is more frightening than any madness. He smiles. He is not surprised to see you.

npcs.name: Halaster Blackcloak
npcs.dialogue: Finally. Do you know how long I have waited? Of course you do not. You think you came here of your own will. You think you fought your way through twenty-three levels of dungeon by accident. Nothing in Undermountain is an accident. Every trap you survived was a test. Every monster you killed was a lesson. Every treasure you found was a bribe to keep you going. And now you are here, and you are ready, and I can finally -- FINALLY -- have a proper conversation. Sit down. We have much to discuss. And yes, this is a trap. But it is a very interesting trap, and I think you will find the bait irresistible.
npcs.notes: Archmage, CR 23. Over 1,000 years old. Not insane -- or rather, his insanity has gone so deep it has come out the other side into a kind of alien clarity. Built Undermountain as a filter to find worthy opponents and successors. Can be fought, reasoned with, or (DC 25 Persuasion) convinced to leave Undermountain entirely. Any outcome he has already predicted and planned for. This does not bother him. It delights him.

loot.name: Undermountain Master Key (opens every lock in the dungeon)

hazards.name: Living spellwork walls (random spell effects each round)
Lair actions: floor mosaic animates, walls discharge spells, gravity reverses

event_triggers: On combat start: Halaster does not stand. He does not draw a weapon. He snaps his fingers, and Undermountain itself fights for him. The walls pulse. The floor shifts. The ceiling opens onto the void above.

On Halaster at half HP: He laughs -- not mockingly, but with genuine delight. 'YES. This is what I built it for. This moment. This exact arrangement of forces. You are MAGNIFICENT.' He stops holding back.

On Halaster at quarter HP: He pauses mid-spell. For one moment, the madness clears entirely. 'I have been alone in this dungeon for a thousand years. Win or lose, thank you for coming.' Then the madness returns, sharper than ever.

On defeat: Halaster falls. The living walls go still. The stars above go dark. For the first time in a millennium, Undermountain is silent. Then, from somewhere far above -- Level 1, Level 2, every level -- doors begin to open. Locks disengage. Traps disarm. The dungeon exhales.

On negotiation success: Halaster stands, dusts off his robes, and walks toward the void. 'A thousand years is enough. I leave you my dungeon, my library, my reputation, and my very sincere recommendation that you do not make the same mistakes I did.' He steps into the stars and is gone. The Undermountain Master Key appears on the throne.
